9	// Each Section contains Options that are used by both the Git plumbing
10	// and the porcelains.
11	// Sections can be further divided into subsections. To begin a subsection
12	// put its name in double quotes, separated by space from the section name,
13	// in the section header, like in the example below:
14	//
15	//	[section "subsection"]

17	// All the other lines (and the remainder of the line after the section header)

19	// which is a short-hand to say that the variable is the boolean "true").

2. *git fsck output check* — line ~693 uses bytes.Contains(fsckOut, []byte("error")). git fsck's exit code already covers the failure case (the err != nil branch fires above it). The substring belt-and-braces could false-positive on benign output containing "error" (e.g. paths/branches with that substring in dangling-warning lines). Consider checking line prefixes (error:, fatal:) or just trusting exit code.

Committed as `7c1a7df`. Summary:

- **`pickHEAD`** (new helper): server-advertised HEAD → `refs/heads/main` → `refs/heads/master` → lexicographically first branch → empty. Resolves via `DesiredRef.TargetRef` so user-supplied ref mappings are honored (a latent bug in the previous code, which used the source ref name).
- **HEAD-setting site in `Run`**: collapsed to a single call into the helper.
- **Test**: `TestPickHEAD` covers all selection paths (advertised wins, ref-mapping respect, main/master fallback, deterministic sort, tags-only no-op, empty desired).

### Style / nits

8. *signBranchTips writes to os.Stderr directly* (cmd.Stdout = os.Stderr line ~596) rather than req.Out (the upper layer threads req.Out everywhere else for status lines). Tests pass Out: io.Discard to silence the run, but signBranchTips's subprocess stderr will still leak. Probably intentional (gpg/ssh-agent prompts must be visible), but worth a comment that this is deliberate.

9. *SignaturesStripped counts double for commits with both Signature and SignatureSHA256* — and also counts each mergetag header. The summary line says "GPG signature(s)" which is a slight overcount/mislabel. Cosmetic.

10. *writeOriginNotes's wrapper commit uses time.Now()* for the committer timestamp, which is why the notes-ref hash is non-deterministic across runs. Documented under "One-off, not incremental" — fine, but consider a fixed/zero timestamp (or SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH) for reproducibility.

11. *writeLoose uses default zlib compression*; git uses level 1 for loose objects historically. Larger files but readable. Cosmetic.

12. *encodeBody doc* (sha256convert.go:1179-1184) says "the hasher we use to capture bytes is irrelevant; we only read the body back out" — the plumbing.FromObjectFormat(formatcfg.SHA1) argument is just because NewMemoryObject requires some format. 1092		body, err := io.ReadAll(r)
1093		if err != nil {
1094			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read encoded body: %w", err)
1095		}

1101	// with SHA1. Atomic via tempfile+rename, idempotent on duplicate hashes.
1102	func (t *translator) writeLoose(typ plumbing.ObjectType, body []byte) (plumbing.Hash, error) {
1103		h := sha256.New()
1104		header := append(typ.Bytes(), ' ')
1105		header = strconv.AppendInt(header, int64(len(body)), 10)
1106		header = append(header, 0)
1107		h.Write(header)
1108		h.Write(body)

Committed as `9d7c494`. Summary of the five nit fixes:

- **#8 signBranchTips**: comment now explicitly explains the `os.Stderr` choice is a deliberate departure from `req.Out` so gpg/ssh-agent/pinentry get a real TTY.
- **#9 signature counting**: `Signature` and `SignatureSHA256` are coalesced (same logical signature in dual-hash repos). Warning label updated to "signature(s) / mergetag header(s)" — drops "GPG" since SSH/X.509 are also possible and acknowledges that mergetags are distinct signed artifacts.
- **#10 writeOriginNotes**: `notesCommitTime` honors `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH`, otherwise pins to the Unix epoch → identical notes-ref hash across runs over the same source.
- **#11 writeLoose**: switched to `zlib.BestSpeed` to match `core.looseCompression` default in git.
- **#12 encodeBody doc**: replaced the cryptic "hasher we use to capture bytes is irrelevant" with an explicit note that the format arg is unused.

- P2: --exclude-ref-prefix can exclude branches and tags, despite the new command saying branches/tags are always converted. Run passes ExcludeRefPrefixes straight into planner.BuildDesiredRefs at cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert/sha256convert.go:238, and the
    planner applies it to branch and tag selection too. That lets a user run a partial conversion and strand cross-branch message rewrites, which is exactly the invariant the command docs are trying to protect.

Committed as `e050592`. Summary:

- **`protectedExcludePrefixes` helper** (cmd/git-sync/internal/sha256convert/sha256convert.go): returns the subset of user-supplied prefixes that — under `planner.IsRefExcluded`'s `HasPrefix` semantics — would catch any `refs/heads/*` or `refs/tags/*`. Covers all the edge cases: bare `""`, `"refs/"`, partial `"refs/h"`, whole namespace, and any sub-namespace.
- **`Run`**: validates upfront and returns an error naming the offending values; the planner is now only ever given prefixes that subtract from `--all-refs`.
- **`convert_sha256.go` help text**: now mentions the rejection so the contract is visible at `--help`.
- **Tests**: `TestProtectedExcludePrefixes` (13 cases covering benign + banned shapes, duplicates, whitespace) and `TestRun_RejectsExcludePrefixesThatDropBranchesOrTags` (end-to-end check that `Run` refuses before any I/O).

1. **Pre-flight collision check**: refuse if source has `refs/notes/sha1-origin` (when origin notes will be written) or `refs/tags/converted/*` (when `--sign` is on).
2. **`runChecks` skip-set**: skip only the specific side-output refs we actually wrote, not by prefix.

695		worktree := filepath.Join(root, "work")
696		dstDir := filepath.Join(root, "target.git")
697	
698		mustGit(t, root, "init", "--bare", srcBare)
699		mustGit(t, root, "init", "-b", "main", worktree)
700		mustGit(t, worktree, "config", "user.name", "convert-sha256 test")
701		mustGit(t, worktree, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
702		mustWrite(t, filepath.Join(worktree, "README"), "hello\n")
703		mustGit(t, worktree, "add", "README")
704		mustGit(t, worktree, "commit", "-m", "initial")
705		// Capture the first commit's SHA1 so the second commit's message can
706		// reference it (both full and abbreviated). The conversion should
707		// rewrite both to the new SHA256 hash.
708		firstSHA1 := strings.TrimSpace(mustGitOutput(t, worktree, "rev-parse", "HEAD"))
709		mustWrite(t, filepath.Join(worktree, "second.txt"), "world\n")
710		mustGit(t, worktree, "add", "second.txt")
711		mustGit(t, worktree, "commit", "-m",
712			fmt.Sprintf("second\n\nreverts %s\nsee short %s", firstSHA1, firstSHA1[:7]))
713		mustGit(t, worktree, "tag", "-a", "v1", "-m", "first tag")
714		mustGit(t, worktree, "remote", "add", "origin", srcBare)
715		mustGit(t, worktree, "push", "origin", "HEAD:refs/heads/main")
716		mustGit(t, worktree, "push", "origin", "v1")
717	
718		srv := newCGIBackend(t, gitBin, root)
719		defer srv.Close()
720	
721		mappingPath := filepath.Join(root, "mapping.tsv")
722		res, err := Run(context.Background(), Request{
723			SourceURL:   srv.URL + "/source.git",
724			TargetDir:   dstDir,
725			MappingFile: mappingPath,
726			Check:       true,
727			Out:         io.Discard,
728		})
729		if err != nil {
730			t.Fatalf("convert-sha256 run: %v", err)
731		}
732		if res.Counts.Commits < 2 {
733			t.Errorf("expected at least 2 commits converted, got %+v", res.Counts)
734		}
735		if res.Counts.Tags != 1 {
736			t.Errorf("expected 1 tag converted, got %d", res.Counts.Tags)
737		}
738		if res.RefsConverted < 2 {
739			t.Errorf("expected at least 2 refs (main + v1), got %d", res.RefsConverted)
740		}
741	
742		// The converted repo must be self-consistent under SHA256.
743		fsckOut, err := exec.CommandContext(t.Context(), gitBin, "-C", dstDir, "fsck", "--full").CombinedOutput()
744		if err != nil {
745			t.Fatalf("git fsck failed: %v\n%s", err, fsckOut)
746		}
747		if strings.Contains(string(fsckOut), "error") || strings.Contains(string(fsckOut), "bad sha") {
748			t.Fatalf("git fsck reported errors:\n%s", fsckOut)
749		}
750	
751		// Sanity: extensions.objectformat is set, and git can walk the history.
752		format := mustGitOutput(t, dstDir, "config", "extensions.objectformat")
753		if strings.TrimSpace(format) != "sha256" {
754			t.Errorf("extensions.objectformat: got %q, want %q", strings.TrimSpace(format), "sha256")
755		}
756		log := mustGitOutput(t, dstDir, "log", "--oneline", "refs/heads/main")
757		if !strings.Contains(log, "initial") || !strings.Contains(log, "second") {
758			t.Errorf("git log missing expected commit subjects:\n%s", log)
759		}
760		tagShow := mustGitOutput(t, dstDir, "cat-file", "-p", "refs/tags/v1")
761		if !strings.Contains(tagShow, "first tag") {
762			t.Errorf("annotated tag did not round-trip:\n%s", tagShow)
763		}
764	
765		// Message rewriting: the second commit's body referenced firstSHA1
766		// twice (full + 7-char short). Both should now be SHA256 hashes.
767		if res.MessageRewrites != 2 {
768			t.Errorf("message rewrites: got %d, want 2", res.MessageRewrites)
769		}
770		secondMsg := mustGitOutput(t, dstDir, "log", "-1", "--format=%B", "refs/heads/main")
771		if strings.Contains(secondMsg, firstSHA1) {
772			t.Errorf("second commit message still contains the original SHA1:\n%s", secondMsg)
773		}
774	
775		// Origin notes: the ref exists, and the head commit's note resolves
776		// to the original SHA1 it was rewritten from.

content.text:
Committed as `635a94b`. Summary:

- **`checkSideOutputCollision`** (new helper): refuses upfront when the source has `refs/notes/sha1-origin` (and `--no-origin-notes` is *not* set) or any `refs/tags/converted/*` (when `--sign` is on). Errors name the offending refs and point at the escape hatches (`--no-origin-notes`, `--exclude-ref-prefix`, dropping `--sign`).
- **`runChecks`**: signature gained `sideOutputs map[plumbing.ReferenceName]struct{}`. Caller passes the *exact* set of refs it wrote (origin notes ref + signed tags), so the refs check no longer prefix-matches `refs/notes/sha1-origin` / `refs/tags/converted/` and never silently hides a legitimate source ref.
- **Tests**: `TestCheckSideOutputCollision` (6 cases: no-collision baseline, origin-notes collision with and without `--no-origin-notes`, converted-tag collision with and without `--sign`, multi-collision sorted output).
